items.name: Everything Hallelujah - Justin Bieber
items.notes: MASSIVE trend on TikTok & Instagram. Bieber's Coachella set sparked it. People film ordinary gratitude moments cutting on 'hallelujah' beats. Controversy: some Christians say it misuses a sacred word. PERFECT for Clem — content angle: 'what if we meant hallelujah literally?' or gratitude-meets-faith content.

items.name: Mozart Lacrimosa (Caffeine/Motivation)
items.notes: Classical sound repurposed for 'getting my life together' / 'caffeine running through my veins' motivational clips. Adaptable for Clem: spiritual discipline, morning prayer routine, 'divine order' energy.

items.name: For Christ Alone - 2819 Church
items.notes: Atlanta-based 2819 Church's worship sound went viral on IG & TikTok, debuted #33 on Billboard Hot Christian Songs. Featured by The Shade Room. Direct church/music intersection — Clem could react/cover/remix.

items.name: Loving Life Again - Ella Langley
items.notes: Country crossover trend — people sharing moments of life getting good again. Adaptable angle: spiritual recovery, finding God after dark seasons, 'loving life again because of grace'.

items.name: Manifest Miracles (Attraction 432 Hz)
items.notes: Spiritual/manifestation audio trending on TikTok. 432 Hz frequency sound used for 'elevate your vibration' content. Intersection with psychedelic/spiritual community. Clem could do a faith-based spin on the manifestation conversation.

items.name: And Emily… That's All (Devil Wears Prada)
items.notes: Comparison trend using Miranda Priestly energy. Adaptable: 'church girlies vs. spiritual-but-not-religious' or 'before I found God vs. after' comparison format.

items.name: It's Gonna Be May - NSYNC
items.notes: Annual May meme trend. Quick-hit seasonal content. Could pair with 'new season, new calling' messaging.

notes: May 2026 TikTok trending sounds scan — focus on spirituality/church/psychedelic crossover angles

items.name: #neurodivergent
items.notes: Massive ongoing tag. Bustle article April 2026 covers how autism terms went mainstream on TikTok. Clem angle: neurodivergent faith experiences, church accessibility, how neurodivergent people experience God differently.

items.name: #autistic
items.notes: Billions of views. The TikTok-ification of autism language is a cultural moment. Content angle: autistic faith, stimming in worship, sensory-friendly church.

items.name: #psychedelics
items.notes: Major tag for Clem's core audience. Intersection with #psychedelicchurch, #psychedelicintegration, #microdosing.

items.notes: Huge spiritual content tag. Clem can own the 'spiritual awakening meets Jesus' lane — what does awakening look like in a church context?

items.notes: Directly relevant to Clem's niche. Growing tag as psychedelic churches gain media attention (Church of Cosmic Consciousness, Sacred Synthesis lawsuit, etc.)

items.name: #churchtok
items.notes: Broader church content community on TikTok. 2819 Church's 'For Christ Alone' is dominating this tag right now.

items.name: #neurodivergentfaith
items.notes: Small but growing. There's a 'Neurodivergent Faith' podcast by NeurodiverseChurch.org. Presbyterian Outlook article April 2026 on welcoming neurodiversity in worship. Clem could own this niche.

items.notes: Using Bieber's 'Everything Hallelujah' audio, creators share 6-10 short clips of small gratitude moments. PERFECT for Clem: 'What if hallelujah meant something real?' faith-forward version. Some Christian creators already criticizing secular use — Clem could bridge the gap.

items.notes: Pair dramatic Mozart Lacrimosa with 'getting my life together' clips. Adaptable: spiritual discipline, morning devotional routine, 'divine order entering my life' reframing.

items.notes: Two-clip comparison format using Devil Wears Prada audio. Adaptable: 'religious trauma vs. spiritual freedom' or 'organized religion vs. mystical faith' contrast.

items.name: Loving Life Again Reveal
items.notes: Ella Langley's song for sharing comeback stories. Clem angle: 'loving life again after God got ahold of me' testimonial format.

items.notes: Retro visual aesthetic trending across lifestyle content. Pair with nostalgic/spiritual content about how faith used to feel vs. now.

items.notes: Massive format across TikTok. Clem angle: 'Day in my life as a psychedelic church pastor' or 'spiritual morning routine' content.

items.notes: Core audience tag. People identifying as spiritual but rejecting organized religion. Clem's lane: showing how church CAN work for spiritual people.

items.notes: Integration-focused tag for people processing psychedelic experiences. Clem's direct niche — what does integration look like in a faith context?

items.notes: Small but growing community. NeurodiverseChurch.org podcast, Presbyterian Outlook article. Clem could become a leading voice here.

items.name: Rev. Megan Hollaway / Ligare
items.notes: Episcopal priest in Pasadena, CA. Featured in UnHerd article 'Meet the psychedelic Christians.' Leading voice in psychedelic Christianity. Direct relevance to Clem's niche.

items.name: 2819 Church / 2819 Worship
items.notes: Atlanta church whose 'For Christ Alone' went viral. Just signed with Reach Records. Currently dominating church TikTok/Instagram. Clem could react/cover/collaborate.

items.name: KNGDM REVIVAL
items.notes: NJ husband-wife EDM duo blending Bible verses with electronic beats. Just signed to Dream Records May 2026. Released 'Psalm 139' and 'Woman At The Well'. Church-meets-EDM space. Clem's niche intersection.

items.name: Hunt Priest / Ligare
items.notes: Another Ligare-featured psychedelic Christian voice. Part of the psychedelic clergy movement gaining mainstream coverage.

items.name: Church of Cosmic Consciousness
items.notes: SF-based psychedelic mushroom church. Gaining media attention as psychedelic churches proliferate. Part of NPR's coverage of 135K+ members in psychedelic religious groups.

items.name: Neurodivergent Faith Podcast
items.notes: Podcast and community (NeurodiverseChurch.org) exploring intersection of neurodivergence and Christian faith. Growing niche. Clem could collaborate or create parallel content.

notes: May 2026 YouTube creator scan — priority creators in psychedelic/church/neurodivergent space

items.name: Bieber's Everything Hallelujah breaks Christian charts
items.notes: Bieber's song hit Billboard Hot Christian Songs Top 10 for the first time. Worship Leader Magazine ran a theology piece. Christian creators on TikTok are both embracing and critiquing the trend. Massive content opportunity for Clem.

items.name: Psychedelic Christianity goes mainstream
items.notes: UnHerd article 'Meet the psychedelic Christians' featuring Rev. Megan Hollaway. NPR reporting 135K+ members in psychedelic religious groups. This IS Clem's niche — he should be making content about this moment.

items.name: Lacrimosa dominates TikTok motivation
items.notes: Mozart's Lacrimosa is THE motivational sound of May 2026. GenZ.ai calls it the defining lifestyle trend. Reframe: 'what if this spiritual energy was pointed at something real?'

items.name: Church of Sacred Synthesis defamation lawsuit dismissed
items.notes: Texas judge dismissed the Church of Sacred Synthesis's (fka Church of Psilomethoxin) defamation case. The church sued multiple psychedelic orgs for calling their sacrament fake. DoubleBlind Mag covered it. Ongoing drama in the psychedelic church space.

items.name: Hallelujah trend Christian backlash debate
items.notes: Distractify article on Christian TikTok backlash to the secular use of 'hallelujah'. Prophetess Jasmine and others saying it's being misused. PERFECT controversy for Clem to engage — the 'what does hallelujah actually mean?' conversation.

items.name: Neurodiversity in worship movement
items.notes: Presbyterian Outlook article on welcoming neurodiversity in worship (April 2026). Midwest churches expanding autism-friendly services. Growing conversation Clem should be part of.

items.name: 2819 Worship signs to Reach Records
items.notes: Atlanta's 2819 Church's worship team signs with Reach Records (Lecrae's label). 'For Christ Alone' debuts #33 on Billboard Hot Christian Songs. Church music industry is shifting — Clem's content should track this.

items.name: KNGDM REVIVAL brings Bible to EDM
items.notes: NJ duo signing to Dream Records, releasing Bible-based EDM tracks (Psalm 139, Woman At The Well). JubileeCast and JesusFreakHideout coverage. Direct intersection of church + altered states (through music).

items.name: Autism language TikTok-ification
items.notes: Bustle article April 2026: neurotypical creators freely using terms like stimming, hyperfixation, overstimulated. Cultural conversation about neurodivergent language going mainstream. Clem angle: how does this intersect with faith communities?

notes: May 2026 general topic scan — cultural moments relevant to Clem's content domains
